title: tephrosia coerulea
intro: Tephrosia coerulea, also known as the blue pea shrub or false indigo, is a flowering plant in the legume family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as alleviating symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Tephrosia coerulea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and capsules, and is also used in traditional Indian medicine, Ayurveda, where it is valued for its adaptogenic and anti-diabetic properties.
